response.justification: The candidate has a broad and genuinely impressive background in distributed systems, testing culture, and full-stack engineering across many environments. However, the role's hard minimum of 8 years of C++ programming is a near-total mismatch — C++ appears only in a short stint at National Instruments (~6 months) and a bachelor's thesis side project, and is conspicuously absent from the candidate's own stated skills list. There is also no evidence of formal technical leadership over cloud or infrastructure teams, and no production-level cloud compute platform architecture experience. With the two most critical dimensions both severely unmet, this profile is not suitable for the role as defined.
strengths: Consistent TDD and software craftsmanship culture demonstrated across multiple roles and through community workshops and open-source tooling
Real high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Broad infrastructure exposure spanning Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, and CI/CD pipelines across multiple companies and a decade of work
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) against the 8-year hard minimum — C++ is not even listed in the candidate's own skills section

No formal technical lead or engineering management role on record — staff-level titles held at two companies but no explicit team ownership, roadmap responsibility, or mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers
No production-level cloud compute platform work; AWS CDK and Kubernetes usage are incidental support roles rather than core platform engineering or architecture ownership
